user_outcome: Public review and adjudication commands atomically persist, commit and push lifecycle transitions, recover without redispatch, and revalidate every S01–S12 bootstrap record before automation becomes authoritative.
rationale: This is the mandatory boundary between manually governed v0.15 bootstrap evidence and Sworn-owned automation. C-10 gives one stateful operation ownership of semantic identity, dispatch, append-only records, the local commit, the exact owner-track push, and restart recovery.
in_scope: Expose sworn maintainability review as the sole public review operation, accepting only Implementer preflight, Implementer closure, and fresh Verifier authoritative role-phase pairs and deriving slice, owner track/ref, cycle, semantic scope, invocation identity, fingerprint, phase eligibility, and dispatch budget from committed authority.

Expose sworn maintainability adjudicate as the sole public Coach transition, accepting only resume_in_scope or re_slice and deriving the eligible cycle-0 authoritative failure and permitted touchpoints from committed reports and status.

Require a clean starting tree and index, validate C-03 through C-09 before side effects, write the full report, compact status ledger, and journal transition coherently, create one decision-bearing commit, and push the exact owner track ref before protocol success; a valid FAIL is also committed and pushed before its non-zero result.

Recover a commit-without-push rerun by revalidating and reusing the exact committed report and transition with zero model dispatch, then complete only the missing exact-ref push; reject partial, rewritten, divergent, or caller-selected evidence.

Run an explicit engine-cutover validator over the committed S01–S12 specs, statuses, journals, reports, semantic identities, transitions, and Git histories and refuse automated authority until every bootstrap artefact passes the new engine unchanged.

Provide structured fail-closed exit behavior and redacted diagnostics; expose no dry-run, no-push, force, waiver, lifecycle override, user-supplied fingerprint, report path, cycle, authority ref, or timestamp.
out_of_scope: Implementer, Verifier, router, and loop adapter adoption belongs to S14-role-lifecycle-recovery.
Track, release, ship, and re-plan adapter adoption belongs to S15-unified-track-merge through S17-engine-replan-migration.
The engine does not repair or synthesize any S01–S12 bootstrap report; a mismatch returns to the original slice authority.
The maintainability command does not merge, deploy, mark shipped, invent Coach decisions, or broaden permitted touchpoints.

effort_complexity.rationale: The public stateful operation owns model dispatch, immutable Git/report identity, multi-file commits, remote durability, exact recovery, and the self-bootstrap cutover.

risks.description: A crash between report creation, status mutation, commit, and push creates ambiguous evidence or causes an expensive duplicate model call.
risks.mitigation: Define deterministic transaction checkpoints, pin every identity in the commit, and allow restart reuse only when the complete committed transition exactly validates; otherwise fail closed.

risks.description: The new engine certifies its own bootstrap by accepting records that differ from the manually governed evidence.
risks.mitigation: Cutover is read-only over S01–S12 and requires exact validation of live committed artefacts and histories; any mismatch is repaired and reverified under the original slice, never rewritten by S13.

risks.description: A caller-controlled option bypasses Baton's role, phase, authority, or durability budget.
risks.mitigation: Expose only the exact role/phase and Coach decision vocabulary; derive all other authority inputs and reject unknown or override-like flags before dispatch or mutation.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built binary runs sworn maintainability review for an eligible Implementer preflight or closure or fresh Verifier authoritative phase on a clean owner-track worktree THE SYSTEM SHALL derive C-03 through C-06 inputs from committed state, perform at most the phase's one permitted dispatch, validate the full report, atomically write report/status/journal, commit them, push the exact track ref, and return success only for PASS (C-10, N-04, N-05, N-10).

WHEN the same command receives a schema-valid maintainability FAIL THE SYSTEM SHALL append its blocking finding identities, commit and push the coherent transition before returning non-zero, SHALL NOT convert it to BLOCKED, and SHALL enforce the exact C-06 needs_coach or re_slice_required next state for the current cycle (C-05, C-06, C-10, N-05).

WHEN a review transition commit exists locally but its exact owner-track push previously failed THE SYSTEM SHALL validate and reuse that committed report and transition, perform zero model dispatches, push only that commit to the expected ref, and produce the same protocol result; IF any report, status, journal, ref, or blob identity differs THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL fail before push (C-05, C-10, N-04, N-10).

WHEN the built binary runs sworn maintainability adjudicate resume_in_scope for an eligible cycle-0 authoritative failure THE SYSTEM SHALL commit and push one C-06 cycle-1 resume with the exact report identities and permitted existing touchpoints; WHEN it runs re_slice THE SYSTEM SHALL commit and push re_slice_required without inventing a rollback slice or replacement scope (C-06, C-10, N-05).

IF review receives an invalid role-phase pair, exhausted dispatch budget, dirty tree or index, stale or divergent owner ref, invalid protocol/report/lifecycle/fingerprint, or any dry-run, no-push, force, waiver, lifecycle-override, caller-selected-cycle, caller-selected-report, or caller-selected-authority option THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL exit non-zero with zero model dispatches and leave refs, index, worktree, reports, status, and journal unchanged (C-03, C-10, N-05, N-09).

WHEN the S13 cutover validator runs against the committed release authority THE SYSTEM SHALL revalidate every S01–S12 spec, status, report, report blob OID, semantic fingerprint, lifecycle transition, evidence interval, rollback fact, and protocol marker without changing a byte; IF any item cannot be reproduced exactly THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL block automated authority and identify the owning original slice (C-03 through C-10, N-09, N-10).
